verses.1: “I have made a covenant with my eyes; How then could I look at a virgin?
verses.2: “And what is the portion of God from above, Or the inheritance of the Almighty from on high?
verses.3: “Is it not disaster to the criminal, And misfortune to those who practice injustice?
verses.4: “Does He not see my ways, And count all my steps?
verses.5: “If I have walked with deception, And my foot has hurried after deceit,
verses.6: Let Him weigh me with accurate scales, And let God know my integrity.
verses.7: “If my step has turned from the way, Or my heart followed my eyes, Or if any spot has stuck to my hands,
verses.8: Let me sow and another eat, And let my crops be uprooted.
verses.9: “If my heart has been enticed by a woman, Or I have lurked at my neighbor’s doorway,
verses.10: May my wife grind grain for another, And let others kneel down over her.
verses.11: “For that would be a lustful crime; Moreover, it would be wrongdoing punishable by judges.
verses.12: “For it would be fire that consumes to Abaddon, And would uproot all my increase.
verses.13: “If I have rejected the claim of my male or female slaves When they filed a complaint against me,
verses.14: What then could I do when God arises? And when He calls me to account, how am I to answer Him?
verses.15: “Did He who made me in the womb not make him, And the same one create us in the womb?
verses.16: “If I have kept the poor from their desire, Or have caused the eyes of the widow to fail,
verses.17: Or have eaten my morsel alone, And the orphan has not shared it
verses.18: (But from my youth he grew up with me as with a father, And from my infancy I guided her),
verses.19: If I have seen anyone perish for lack of clothing, Or that the needy had no covering,
verses.20: If his waist has not thanked me, And if he has not been warmed with the fleece of my sheep,
verses.21: If I have lifted up my hand against the orphan, Because I saw I had support in the gate,
verses.22: May my shoulder fall from its socket, And my arm be broken off at the elbow.
verses.23: “For disaster from God is a terror to me, And because of His majesty I can do nothing.
verses.24: “If I have put my confidence in gold, And called fine gold my trust,
verses.25: If I have gloated because my wealth was great, And because my hand had obtained so much;
verses.26: If I have looked at the sun when it shone, Or the moon going in splendor,
verses.27: And my heart was secretly enticed, And my hand threw a kiss from my mouth,
verses.28: That too would have been a guilty deed calling for judgment, For I would have denied God above.
verses.29: “Have I rejoiced at the misfortune of my enemy, Or become excited when evil found him?
verses.30: “No, I have not allowed my mouth to sin By asking for his life in a curse.
verses.31: “Have the people of my tent not said, ‘Who can find one who has not been satisfied with his meat’?
verses.32: “The stranger has not spent the night outside, For I have opened my doors to the traveler.
verses.33: “Have I covered my wrongdoings like a man, By hiding my guilt in my shirt pocket,
verses.34: Because I feared the great multitude And the contempt of families terrified me, And I kept silent and did not go out of doors?
verses.35: “Oh that I had one to hear me! Here is my signature; Let the Almighty answer me! And the indictment which my adversary has written,
verses.36: I would certainly carry it on my shoulder, I would tie it to myself like a garland.
verses.37: “I would declare to Him the number of my steps; Like a prince, I would approach Him.
verses.38: “If my land cries out against me, And its furrows weep together;
verses.39: If I have eaten its fruit without money, Or have caused its owners to lose their lives,
verses.40: May the thorn-bush grow instead of wheat, And stinkweed instead of barley.” The words of Job are ended.
